Scenario: In-term resumption within a single billed period should not create a manual credit note
Expected billing effect: No manual credit note because Chargebee should handle the in-term proration
Calculation explanation: Pause start and resume date fall in the same billed term

Scenario: Out-of-term resumption on a payment_due start invoice should reduce that earlier invoice
Expected billing effect: Creates adjustment credit against the pause-start invoice

Scenario: Out-of-term resumption from a month-crossing billed period credits only the paused days in the start invoice
Expected billing effect: Creates refundable credit against the pause-start invoice

Scenario: In-term resumption inside a month-crossing billed period should not create a manual credit note
Expected billing effect: No manual credit note because Chargebee should handle the in-term proration
Calculation explanation: Pause start and resume date both fall in the same billed term

Scenario: Out-of-term resumption credits the pause-start invoice rather than the later resume invoice
Expected billing effect: Creates refundable credit against the pause-start invoice

Scenario: Out-of-term resumption for a lower monthly recurring revenue credits the earlier invoice only
Expected billing effect: Creates refundable credit against the pause-start invoice

Scenario: Out-of-term resumption for a higher monthly recurring revenue credits only the paused days inside the start invoice
Expected billing effect: Creates refundable credit against the pause-start invoice

Scenario: Out-of-term resumption for a mid-range MRR credits the overlap of the pause with the start invoice
Expected billing effect: Creates refundable credit against the pause-start invoice
